mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60 percent of triggered cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending profit margin compression, causing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of late-cycle periods when industrial production declines.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window or payroll growth shows no predictive power for equity direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate above 2.0 percent triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward fixed income, generating negative returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 55 percent of instances when the federal funds rate crosses the 2.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates sustainable demand expansion without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, supporting positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy or produces negative returns in over 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales following a macroeconomic shock signals rapid consumer recovery and earnings rebound, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of post-shock periods despite retail sales acceleration.
